summary: Verified Emergent auth error plumbing: backend returns 401 with detail starting 'emergent_404:', logs full session_id, Login banner shows decoded auth_error, AuthCallback redirects with emergent_404 (not 'unknown'), and exactly 1 POST /api/auth/session under StrictMode.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is ~1190 lines — consider splitting into routers (auth, workers, subcontractors, reports, insights) for maintainability.
emergent_<code> detail body is capped to 200 chars via [:200] slice — long Emergent errors may be truncated in UI; consider raising or using the parsed 'detail' field.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Auth error transparency verified end-to-end. Curl: POST /api/auth/session {session_id: bogus_zzz_test} -> 401 with detail 'emergent_404: {...user_data_not_found...}'. Backend logs at /var/log/supervisor/backend.err.log contain full session_id. Frontend Login banner [data-testid=auth-error-banner] shows on ?auth_error=... . AuthCallback #session_id=... redirects to /?auth_error=emergent_404:%20... (no 'unknown'). Exactly 1 POST under StrictMode confirmed via module-level inFlight guard.
rca of the issue: N/A — this iteration only validates error plumbing; actual Google OAuth cannot be exercised in headless browser.
